title: Writing - Part 1: Gender Roles
description: Your class has been discussing how gender roles have changed in modern society.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the changes in your notes. You should explain which change you think has been most significant, giving reasons for your opinion.

changes: More women in professional and leadership roles
Men taking a more active role in childcare
Greater awareness of gender stereotypes in media
opinions: True equality between men and women has still not been achieved.
Children benefit greatly when fathers are more involved in their upbringing.
The media still reinforces harmful stereotypes despite some progress.

structure.introduction: Introduce changing gender roles in society and the two changes you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first change (e.g., more women in professional roles) and why it is significant.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second change (e.g., men taking an active role in childcare) and its impact on families.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both changes and state which you think has been most significant.

content.modelAnswer:
Gender roles have shifted dramatically over the past century, and the pace of change has accelerated in recent decades. What was once considered natural and fixed is now increasingly seen as a social construction open to question and revision. This essay will consider the growing presence of women in professional life and the changing role of fathers in the home.

The increase in women occupying senior positions in business, politics and other professions is one of the most visible social changes of our time. Female leaders serve as powerful role models and challenge the assumption that certain roles are inherently male. Although inequality persists in many sectors, the progress made in the past fifty years has been remarkable.

Equally significant is the shift in how fatherhood is perceived and practised. Increasingly, men are expected and encouraged to be active caregivers rather than just breadwinners. Research suggests that children with highly involved fathers develop stronger social and academic skills, suggesting that this change benefits the whole family.

In conclusion, while women's progress in the professional world is undeniably important, I believe that greater male involvement in childcare is the most transformative change. It challenges the deepest assumptions about gender and has a direct, daily impact on the lives of children and families. When fathers and mothers share responsibilities equally, both benefit from a fairer and more balanced way of life.
